title: iberis saxatilis subsp. pseudosaxatilis
intro: Iberis saxatilis subsp. pseudosaxatilis, also known as rock candytuft, is a flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of isothiocyanates. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, such as its ability to reduce fever, treat respiratory issues, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of rock candytuft are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various health conditions.
